gaps: asr: engine invariants R2/R3/R4/KV-overflow not observable on 'paraformer_trt' (qwen3-asr edgellm runtime is v0.8.0-only); corpus transcript baseline captured instead
tts/customvoice: CustomVoice (zh/en 9-row prefix) not deployed (live backend is 'matcha_trt'); no CustomVoice golden
tts/moss: MOSS-TTS-Nano not deployed (live backend is 'matcha_trt'); no MOSS golden
notes: READ-ONLY capture: no container was stopped/restarted. seeed-voice stayed 'Up' across the entire run.

TTS ASR-roundtrip uses the /asr/stream WebSocket. The offline 'POST /asr' endpoint is BROKEN in :prod-unified-v8 (server main.py:2228 builds the response with '**result.meta' but result.meta is None -> TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not a mapping -> HTTP 500). This is a pre-existing v0.7.1 service bug, unrelated to ASR/TTS engine quality.

build.log_error_count (30) consists of those same 'POST /asr' 500 tracebacks left in the rolling docker-log tail by the harness's own first-pass roundtrip probes (later switched to the WS path). They are NOT engine/CUDA faults. The build gate's primary signal is engine-MD5 drift; treat this log count as a known-benign artifact of the broken offline endpoint, not a v0.8.0 regression signal.

matcha_trt is NOT byte-deterministic even at N=1 (bf16 TRT): same prompt twice yields different PCM MD5, same byte length, ~equal RMS. The N=2 slot-pool gate therefore uses energy (RMS) parity, not MD5 parity, for this backend (recorded as deterministic=false in tts/n2_slotpool.json). A deterministic engine (e.g. MOSS) would be gated on MD5.
